title: 10 best herbal creams for oily hair

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage cream contains antioxidants like rosmarinic acid, which help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and lead to oily hair by reducing sebum production.

Astringent properties: Sage's astringent compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help tighten the hair follicle and reduce oil secretion, controlling excess sebum and promoting balanced hair growth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal sage cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, including camphor and borneol, soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and flaking associated with oily hair, while also helping to regulate sebum production.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with oily hair because of its antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to control scalp infections and inflammation. The cream's astringent properties help to tighten the hair follicles, reducing oil production and leaving the hair looking cleaner for longer. Additionally, sage's antioxidant properties help to protect the scalp from environmental stressors, promoting a balanced scalp and reducing oiliness. This leads to a healthier and more manageable hair.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce sebum production and prevent hair follicle blockage, thereby controlling oily hair.
Salvianolic acid: A water-soluble phenolic compound that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help regulate oil production and soothe the scalp, reducing oily hair.

Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid that has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp that contribute to oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to control oil production and soothe the scalp.
Stems: Used for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oiliness and balance the scalp's pH.
Roots: Utilized for their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which help to prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of dried salvia officinalis flowers and leaves and finely grind them using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground salvia officinalis with 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 to 15 minutes or until the beeswax has fully melted.
Stir in 10 milliliters of distilled water and continue heating for another 5 to 7 minutes.
Remove the cream from the heat and allow it to cool and thicken before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm cream's antimicrobial properties help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, reducing oil production and preventing clogged pores.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: The astringent and antiseptic properties of lemon balm cream help to dry up excess oil, reduce inflammation, and prevent further oil buildup by constricting hair follicles and reducing sebum production.

Calming and soothing properties: Lemon balm's calming and soothing properties can help to reduce stress and anxiety-related scalp irritation, which can contribute to oily hair, while also providing relief from itchiness and flakiness associated with dandruff or other scalp conditions.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. The herb's ability to balance the scalp's pH levels and reduce inflammation helps to minimize oil production. This results in a reduction of greasiness and an improvement in the overall appearance and feel of the hair. Additionally, lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to oily hair and scalp conditions.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps to reduce sebum production, thereby controlling oily hair.
Limonene: A terpene that has antifungal and antibacterial properties, helping to prevent scalp infections that can contribute to oily hair.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, which can lead to oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Melissa officinalis leaves are used due to their high concentration of volatile oils, particularly linalool, which help balance scalp pH and reduce oil production.
Flowers: Melissa officinalis flowers are used because they contain antiseptic and antifungal properties that help control scalp infections and reduce flakiness associated with oily hair.
Rhyzomes: Melissa officinalis rhyzomes are used for their astringent properties, which help to tighten pores and reduce oil secretion on the scalp, promoting a balanced hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of melissa officinalis flowers at dawn when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place for 1-2 weeks or until they are completely brittle.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried melissa officinalis flowers in 8 ounces of carrier oil for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the oil and mix it with 2 tablespoons of beeswax to create a smooth consistency.
Add the melissa officinalis infused oil mixture to a double boiler and heat it until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedy_properties: Aloe vera's antibacterial properties: Help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to oiliness and dandruff, by killing off bacteria that feed on sebum.
Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties: Soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing inflammation and redness caused by excessive oil production, while also helping to regulate oil gland function.
Aloe vera's astringent properties: Help to tighten the pores on the scalp, reducing oil leakage and preventing further oil buildup, while also absorbing excess oil and impurities.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with oily hair because of its natural ingredients that balance scalp pH and reduce excess oil production. remedy_constituents: Chromone: Chromone has astringent properties that help to reduce sebum production and control oiliness in the hair.
Aloin: Aloin is a anthraquinone glycoside that has been found to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to regulate the scalp and reduce oil production.
Saponins: Saponins in Aloe vera have a gentle cleansing and balancing effect on the scalp, helping to remove excess oil and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel from Aloe vera leaves helps to balance the scalp's pH and reduce oil production.
Gel from Leaves: The Aloe vera gel is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g aloe vera gel 2 tablespoons coconut oil 2 tablespoons beeswax and 10 drops lavender essential oil for cream base.
Melt 1 tablespoon coconut oil and 1 tablespoon beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 5 minutes.
Combine 100g aloe vera gel with melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Add 10 drops lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir gently for another minute.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Combine 1/4 cup of distilled water with 2 tablespoons of dried rosmarinus officinalis flowers in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the water has reduced slightly.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it steep for 20-30 minutes allowing the flavors to infuse.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl to remove the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and add the infused rosmarinus officinalis mixture to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower cream helps to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the scalp, which can contribute to oil buildup and other issues associated with oily hair.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh echinacea purpurea roots and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned echinacea roots in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the ground echinacea powder with 2 ounces of distilled water in a heat-resistant bowl.
Combine the echinacea mixture with 4 ounces of a carrier cream base such as beeswax and shea butter to create a smooth consistency.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness and swelling in the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair by irritating the skin and causing it to produce even more oil.

remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container to collect the liquid.
Combine 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts completely in 10-15 minutes.
Pour the melted mixture into a clean tin and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 percent of the calendula officinalis flower heads at full bloom for maximum potency in 2-3 weeks.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 60-70 degrees fahrenheit for 7-14 days.
Infuse 1 part dried calendula officinalis flowers in 4 parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and mix 5-10 percent beeswax with the oil to create a stable emulsion in 10 minutes.
Whip the mixture until it reaches the desired consistency then pour into containers for storage.

remedy_constituents: Fatty acids: Fatty acids in Urtica dioica creams may help regulate sebum production, reducing the amount of oil on the scalp and hair.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers in the early morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 2 to 3 weeks.
Steep 1 cup of dried leaves and flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes to make an infusion.
Strain the infusion and mix it with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools to create a smooth and creamy herbal urtica dioica cream.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 bunches of taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers on a sunny morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop 1 cup of the harvested taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers into fine pieces with a sharp knife.
Infuse 1 cup of the chopped taraxacum officinale in 2 cups of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh plant leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm area at 30-40 degrees celsius for 7-10 days.
Grind 10 grams of dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Melt 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius.
Combine the plant powder with 10 grams of coconut oil and the melted beeswax mixture to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with oily hair because of its unique ability to balance sebum production in the scalp. Additionally, fennel's antiseptic properties help to purify the scalp, preventing clogged pores that can contribute to oily hair. remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Grind 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 5 grams of powdered foeniculum vulgare with 100 milliliters of jojoba oil in a glass jar stirring vigorously for 2 minutes.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and heat at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes under gentle stirring.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another glass jar and let it cool to room temperature before storing.

remedy_recipe: Crush 20g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with 100ml of distilled water to create a paste that is allowed to steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Combine the infused liquid with 50g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler heating the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 65 degrees celsius.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before pouring into sterilized tubes or jars for storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow root althaea officinalis and dry it properly for future use.
Combine dried marshmallow root with distilled water in a ratio of one part herb to three parts liquid.
Steep the mixture for 20 minutes at room temperature then strain it using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Mix the herbal infusion with melted sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler setup.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh rosa rugosa petals and dry them in the shade for at least two weeks.
Combine 1 cup of dried rosa rugosa petals with 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after cooling completely.
Add 1/2 cup of distilled water to the infused oil and mix well before whipping until it becomes light and fluffy.
Pour the whipped cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with oily hair because they contain silymarin, a potent antioxidant that helps to regulate sebum production in the scalp. This natural ingredient absorbs excess oil and purifies the hair follicles, reducing greasiness and flakiness. Additionally, milk thistle's anti-inflammatory properties soothe an itchy scalp and calm irritations caused by excessive oil secretion, promoting a healthy and balanced hair environment that looks and feels great. Regular use of these creams can lead to manageable, non-oily locks with a radiant shine.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Helps to regulate sebum production, reducing excess oiliness by inhibiting the activity of the enzyme 5-alpha-reductase, which converts testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to oil production.
Flavonoids: Have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated scalps associated with oily hair, reducing redness and inflammation.
Saponins: Act as natural emulsifiers, helping to break down and dissolve excess sebum and impurities on the scalp, while also cleansing the pores and removing dirt and debris.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of milk thistle are used in creams due to their ability to regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation, making them an effective treatment for oily hair.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing oiliness and flakiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry silybum marianum leaves and flowers in a well-ventilated area over several days to prevent spoilage.
Combine one part dried silybum marianum herb with two parts carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil takes on a dark green color and strong scent.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids, and allow it to cool to room temperature.
Whip the cooled oil mixture with an electric mixer for 2-3 minutes until it thickens to a creamy consistency suitable for hair treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sea buckthorn creams helps with oily hair because of its unique ability to balance the scalp's natural pH level, reducing excess oil production. The cream's antioxidant-rich properties also help to purify and clarify the hair follicles, allowing for a healthy flow of sebum that is not overproduced. Additionally, sea buckthorn's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and redness associated with oily hair. Overall, regular use of herbal sea buckthorn cream can help to regulate oil production, leading to healthier-looking hair that is less greasy and more manageable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sea buckthorn cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to excessive oil production in the scalp and hair.

Regulatory effects on sebum production: The omega-7 fatty acids present in sea buckthorn cream have been shown to regulate sebum production, helping to balance the scalp's natural oils and reduce the appearance of an oily scalp and hair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sea buckthorn cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm irritated scalps and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to oil buildup and dandruff.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sea buckthorn plant contain antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing oil production and soothing scalp irritation.
Fruits: The fruits of the sea buckthorn plant are rich in essential fatty acids and vitamins, which help to nourish and balance the scalp, while also controlling sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried hippophae rhamnoides berries from trusted sources and grind them into fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the ground hippophae rhamnoides powder with 200 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating it slowly over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Add 50 grams of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until melted and fully incorporated after 5 minutes of gentle stirring.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 60°c before adding 20 grams of aloe vera gel and stirring well, taking approximately 2 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars, sealing them tightly and storing in a cool dry place for at least 24 hours before use or distribution within the next 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with oily hair because they possess natural antibacterial and antifungal properties that help to control the production of excess sebum, thereby reducing oiliness. The cream's gentle yet effective ingredients also help to purify the scalp and absorb excess moisture, leaving your hair feeling clean, refreshed, and manageable. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties soothe an itchy or irritated scalp, making it an excellent natural remedy for oily hair woes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginger's antioxidant properties help reduce free radical damage to the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair by promoting inflammation and blocking pores.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce redness and swelling on the scalp, reducing oil production and balancing sebum levels.
Natural Astringent Properties: The astringent properties of ginger help tighten the hair follicles and shrink pores, reducing excess oil secretion and minimizing the appearance of oily roots.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes help to reduce oil production and soothe scalp irritations.
Buds: Containing essential oils with antiseptic and antibacterial properties, buds help to prevent clogged pores and reduce dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out one cup of coconut oil and add two tablespoons of grated fresh ginger root.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl over boiling water for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat and let steep for an additional 30 minutes before straining the oil through cheesecloth.
Combine one cup of beeswax pellets with four tablespoons of coconut oil and one teaspoon of ginger essential oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments stirring between each interval until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent properties that help to balance the scalp's oil production. The antiseptic qualities of lemongrass also help to reduce dandruff and flakiness, which can contribute to an oily appearance. Additionally, the moisturizing benefits of lemongrass cream provide nourishment without weighing hair down, allowing it to absorb excess oil and leave a refreshing, clean feeling that lasts all day.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Lemongrass has antibacterial properties that help control the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to oiliness and itchiness, reducing the risk of infections.
Antifungal property: The antifungal properties in lemongrass creams help combat fungal infections, such as dandruff and ringworm, which can exacerbate oily hair issues.
Astringent property: Lemongrass has astringent properties that help dry up excess oil on the scalp, leaving it feeling cleaner and less greasy.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots have antifungal and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing dandruff and itchiness caused by oily hair.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of lemongrass plant contains citral, a compound that helps to control oil production and reduce acne caused by oily skin.
Barks: Lemongrass bark has been traditionally used to treat scalp issues such as dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema, which can be exacerbated by oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat it for 30 minutes to infuse the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids, and let it cool at room temperature.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of distilled water to the cooled oil and heat it in the double boiler until the wax melts, stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it solidify completely before scooping it into small containers for use as a cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with oily hair because they possess natural astringent and antiseptic properties that work wonders in controlling oil production. The menthol present in peppermint oil helps to dry up excess oil, while its refreshing scent stimulates blood circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth. Additionally, the cream's ability to balance the pH level of the scalp helps to reduce frizz and add shine to oily locks, leaving them looking healthy, manageable, and silky smooth.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial agents: Peppermint's antimicrobial properties help combat scalp infections, dandruff, and other underlying causes of oily hair, reducing oil production and promoting a healthy scalp.

Cooling and soothing effects: The menthol in peppermint cream provides a cooling sensation that can help calm an itchy or irritated scalp, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with oily hair.

Stimulating circulation: Peppermint's stimulating properties increase blood flow to the scalp, which helps to reduce excess oil production by improving overall circulation and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and soothing irritation caused by oily hair.
Stems: The stems of peppermint contain antioxidants and astringent compounds that help to tighten pores and control excess oil production.
remedy_recipe: Combine one cup of distilled water with one tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves in a heatproof bowl.
Steep the peppermint mixture for 15-20 minutes then strain and discard solids.
Melt two tablespoons of coconut oil and four tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler or saucepan.
Add three drops of peppermint essential oil to the melted wax mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the cooled mixture into glass containers and allow to set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for oily hair are natural products that help balance the scalp's oil production, reducing greasiness and preventing hair loss. These creams contain potent herbs like Melaleuca alternifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Aloe barbadensis, Cymbopogon citratus, Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus serpyllum, Aloe vera, Camellia sinensis, and Saponaria officinalis. If left untreated, oily hair can lead to social anxiety, low self-esteem, and even relationship problems due to excessive sweating and hair loss.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily hair that are treated with herbal creams are acne, eczema, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, lavender oil, and chamomile help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin barrier function. These natural remedies also address underlying issues such as fungal infections, allergies, and hormonal imbalances that can exacerbate these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with oily hair is neem and tea tree oil-infused cream. The neem cream helps to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause oiliness, while the tea tree oil cream balances the scalp's pH and reduces oil production. Adding a few drops of lavender oil can also help to calm the scalp and reduce dandruff, making it a powerful combination to keep oily hair under control and promote a healthy scalp. Regular use can lead to a balanced and manageable mane.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties, derived from the tea tree plant. The tea tree oil present in these creams helps to balance the scalp's pH, reduce excess oil production, and eliminate bacteria that can cause oiliness. Additionally, Melaleuca alternifolia creams have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the scalp, reducing irritation and flakiness associated with oily hair. This natural remedy can help to regulate oil production and promote a healthier scalp.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This constituent helps to reduce sebum production and prevent clogged pores, thereby controlling oily hair.
Limonene: Limonene has antiseptic and astringent properties that help to dry out the scalp and reduce oil production, thereby controlling oily hair.
Caryophyllene oxide: This constituent has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth on the scalp, thereby controlling oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are often used due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help control oily hair.
Leaves: The leaves also contain antifungal properties that help prevent scalp infections, which can contribute to oily hair.
Leaves: The tea tree oil from the leaves has a balancing effect on the scalp's pH, helping to regulate oil production and reduce greasiness.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50 grams of melaleuca alternifolia leaves and dry them in a low temperature oven for 2 hours.
Combine 50 grams of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves with 200 ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. The essential oils found in rosemary, such as borneol and bornyl acetate, help to regulate the scalp's oil production, reducing the amount of sebum that is released. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of rosemary help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy balance of oil production. This results in a reduction of oiliness and an improvement in hair texture.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps to reduce sebum production and improve scalp health, which can contribute to controlling oily hair.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the scalp and reduce inflammation that can contribute to oily hair.
Caryophyllene: Acts as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory, helping to control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp that can contribute to oily hair and dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Rosmarinus officinalis are used to make creams for oily hair because of their astringent and antifungal properties that help balance the scalp's pH and reduce excess oil.

Flowers: The flowers of the Rosmarinus officinalis are used to make creams for oily hair because of their antiseptic and antifungal properties that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and oil production.
Stems: The stems of the Rosmarinus officinalis are used to make creams for oily hair because of their antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help purify the scalp and reduce oil production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and dry them in a dehydrator at 45c for 3 hours.
Combine the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 200g of sweet almond oil and infuse for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and combine it with 100g of beeswax and 50g of cocoa butter in a heat-resistant bowl.
Heat the mixture over a double boiler at 60c for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 30 minutes before pouring into containers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with oily hair because they contain the antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of lavender oil, which can help to balance the scalp's natural pH and reduce excess oil production. The calming effects of lavender oil also help to soothe an itchy scalp, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy scalp environment. As a result, the hair appears cleaner for longer and the scalp feels refreshed, reducing the need for frequent washing and helping to regulate oil production.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps to reduce sebum production and regulate the scalp's natural oil balance, leading to a decrease in oily hair.
Linalyl acetate: Exerts a soothing effect on the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to oily hair and dandruff.
Caryophyllene oxide: Demonstrates antimicrobial properties, which can help to control the growth of bacteria and yeast on the scalp that can contribute to oily hair and other scalp issues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their antiseptic and antifungal properties help to balance the scalp's pH and reduce oil production.
Flowers: Their calming and astringent properties help to soothe the scalp and tighten pores, reducing oiliness.
Stems: Their astringent properties help to reduce oil production by tightening the pores on the scalp, promoting a healthier balance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and aroma.
Steep the harvested flowers in 100ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infusion using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids and obtain a clear liquid.
Combine the strained liquid with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer for 5-7 minutes until it thickens and becomes a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with oily hair because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant has the ability to balance the scalp's pH, reducing oil production and preventing excessive greasiness. Additionally, its soothing properties can help calm an itchy scalp, allowing the hair to breathe and reducing the appearance of oily roots. This makes Aloe barbadensis creams a popular natural remedy for controlling oily hair and promoting a healthy scalp.
remedy_constituents: Fatty acids: Help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing oil production and minimizing the appearance of oily hair.
Glycoproteins: Soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to excess oil production and oily hair.
Anthraquinones: Possess astringent properties that help to tighten the skin and reduce sebum production, leading to a decrease in oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera gel, extracted from the leaves, helps to balance the oil production in the scalp and reduce dandruff.
Seeds: Aloe vera seed extract is used to help regulate sebum production and prevent oil buildup on the scalp.
Leaves: Aloe vera extract from the leaves also helps to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize the equipment by washing it with soap and rinsing it with distilled water for 5 minutes.
Combine 1 cup of distilled water with 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and heat the mixture for 10 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the heated mixture and stir until melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and 5 drops of essential oil and mix well.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. The citral content in lemongrass oil, a key component of Cymbopogon citratus, helps balance the scalp's oil production, reducing excessive sebum secretion. This, in turn, prevents oil buildup on the hair shaft, leaving it looking cleaner and healthier. Regular use of these creams can also help to regulate the scalp's natural pH, promoting a healthy and balanced hair growth environment.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Helps regulate sebum production, reducing oiliness and controlling hair's natural shine.
Limonene: Possesses astringent and drying properties, which can help balance the scalp's oil production and reduce hair oiliness.
Geraniol: Exhibits antimicrobial and antifungal properties, preventing scalp infections and inflammation that can lead to increased oil production and other hair issues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, particularly limonene, which helps control oil production and reduce dandruff.
Rhyzomes: They are a primary source of geraniol and other compounds that help balance scalp pH and reduce oiliness.
Stems: They contain compounds like geraniol and limonene, which have antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control scalp infections and reduce oil production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of cymbopogon citratus leaves by cutting them during the early morning hours when the oils are most concentrated.
Steam 250g of the harvested leaves in 1l of distilled water for 20 minutes to release their active compounds.
Combine 200g of the steamed leaves with 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Blend the mixture at 60°c for 15 minutes and then filter it through a cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Store the resulting cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with oily hair because it contains eucalyptol, a natural decongestant and antiseptic that helps to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The cooling and refreshing properties of eucalyptus oil can also help to reduce inflammation and soothe an itchy scalp, creating an environment that is less conducive to excessive oil production. This, in turn, can help to regulate and control oily hair, leaving it looking and feeling healthier and more manageable. Regular use can also improve scalp health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This constituent helps to reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent clogged pores, which can lead to oily hair.
Bornyl acetate: This terpene has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing oil production and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant and antiseptic that helps to balance the scalp's oil production.
Barks: The barks are used as they contain astringent and antiseptic properties that help to reduce oiliness and prevent bacterial growth on the scalp.
Essential Oil (derived from leaves): The essential oil is used as it is rich in antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to control flaking and itching associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 eucalyptus globulus leaves from a trusted supplier or grow your own plants in a well-ventilated area.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150-200 degrees fahrenheit for 1-2 hours.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in 8 ounces of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2-4 weeks.
Filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the eucalyptus globulus solids from the oil.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the eucalyptus globulus infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus serpyllum creams helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. The essential oils present in Thymus serpyllum, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing the production of sebum and controlling oiliness. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of these oils help to eliminate bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to oily hair and scalp issues, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment. This leads to healthier-looking hair.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as an antimicrobial agent, reducing bacteria that can contribute to oily scalp and hair.
Carvacrol: Exhibits antiseptic and astringent properties, helping to balance oil production and reduce greasiness.
Rosmarinic acid: Has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, soothing the scalp and reducing irritation that can lead to excessive oil production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to balance the scalp's pH and reduce oil production.
Roots: They are used due to their ability to stimulate blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the scalp, helping to control oil production.
Flowers: They are used due to their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which help to cleanse the scalp and prevent oil buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus serpyllum leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated completely.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 150°f for 1-2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried thymus serpyllum leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to make a tea.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of the thymus serpyllum tea in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens and forms a smooth creamy texture taking about 5-7 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with oily hair because they have natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These properties help to control the scalp's oil production, reducing the amount of sebum released onto the hair. The soothing effects of aloe vera calm the scalp, preventing irritation and flakiness that can contribute to oily hair. Additionally, aloe vera's moisturizing properties help to balance the scalp's natural moisture levels, promoting a healthy scalp and reducing excess oil production. This leads to cleaner, fresher-looking hair.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds help to reduce oil production in the scalp by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in sebum synthesis.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids, particularly aloe-emodin and aloin, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to control oily hair by reducing the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp.
Anthraquinones: Anthraquinones, particularly aloe-emodin, have been shown to have a drying effect on the scalp, which helps to reduce oil production and alleviate oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in aloe vera gel, which helps to balance the scalp's pH and reduce oil production.
Seeds: They contain aloe-emodin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that contribute to oily hair.
Flowers: They are used to extract aloe vera's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and balance the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Melt 1 cup of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat for 5 minutes.
Combine 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a bowl.
Stir in 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of beeswax into the aloe mixture.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 minutes or until the beeswax melts completely.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and refrigerate for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis creams helps with oily hair because they contain antioxidants and fatty acids that help to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The cream's ability to nourish and moisturize the hair and scalp can reduce the appearance of oily roots, while its astringent properties help to control excess sebum. Additionally, the cream's natural anti-inflammatory properties can soothe an irritated scalp, creating a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing oiliness. This can lead to a balanced and manageable hair texture.
remedy_constituents: Theaflavins: These flavonoids may help regulate sebum production and reduce oiliness in the hair by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in androgen metabolism.

Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds may help control oil production and reduce the appearance of oily hair by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the scalp and reduce inflammation.

Gallic acid: This phenolic acid may help reduce the production of sebum and prevent clogged pores, resulting in a reduction in the appearance of oily hair by its antioxidant and astringent properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of antioxidants and fatty acids, which help to balance the scalp's pH and reduce oil production.
Seeds: They are used because they contain squalene, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing oiliness.
(Optional) Buds: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to regulate the scalp's oil production.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500g of camellia sinensis leaves and 200g of sweet almond oil in a mixing bowl.
Heat 500ml of coconut oil in a double boiler to 40°c and add the camellia sinensis leaves.
Steep the camellia sinensis leaves in the heated oil for 2-3 hours to infuse the mixture.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 200g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter into the infused oil until it reaches a creamy consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis creams helps with oily hair because they contain natural saponins and other plant-derived cleansing agents that effectively remove dirt, oil, and product buildup from the scalp and hair. These gentle yet potent ingredients help balance the scalp's natural pH, reduce oil production, and promote a healthy scalp environment. Regular use of Saponaria officinalis creams can lead to a significant reduction in oiliness, leaving hair looking cleaner, healthier, and more manageable for longer periods.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help to reduce oily hair by creating a soap-like effect on the scalp, effectively removing excess sebum and oil.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to balance the scalp's natural oil production and reduce dandruff, thereby controlling oily hair.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids possess antifungal and antibacterial properties, helping to prevent scalp infections that can lead to oily hair and scalp irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their saponin content, which helps to balance the scalp's natural pH and reduce oil production.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp.
Roots: The roots are used for their rich content of saponins, which help to gently cleanse the scalp and reduce oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried saponaria officinalis flowers and leaves from a trusted source to ensure quality.
Combine the dried herbs with 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan and heat it to 100°f.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes to release the herbal properties into the water.
Mix the cooled herbal infusion with 1 cup of shea butter and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture for 5 minutes until it thickens and reaches a smooth, creamy consistency.
